title: OpenClaw v2026.5.22: 4,100× Faster Gateway, Meeting Notes AI, Claude 4.x
description: OpenClaw v2026.5.22 delivers a 4,100× gateway speedup (20s → 5ms), a new Meeting Notes plugin with Discord voice capture, Claude 4.x 1M-context migration, and 100+ fixes for Telegram, Slack, and WhatsApp channels.
author: PulseAgent
content:
# OpenClaw v2026.5.22: 4,100× Faster Gateway, Meeting Notes AI & Claude 4.x — What It Means for Your B2B Sales Agent

OpenClaw shipped v2026.5.22 on May 24, 2026, and one number dominates the release: **4,100×**. That is the measured speedup for gateway `/models` endpoint responses — from roughly 20 seconds down to 5 milliseconds — thanks to a pre-warmed provider auth-state map. For B2B SDR deployments, this single change eliminates the frustrating cold-start delay that made the first message of every session feel sluggish.

Beyond raw performance, the release adds a purpose-built Meeting Notes plugin, migrates AI backbone to Claude 4.x models with 1M-context GA support, and lands 100+ targeted fixes across Telegram, Slack, WhatsApp, and agent execution paths. Here is everything that matters for your sales automation stack.

## Gateway Performance: 4,100× Is Not a Typo

Previous versions re-read the provider auth-state map on every `/models` call. Under load — or on first boot — this compounded into 15–20 second response times that cascaded into visible lag for end users. v2026.5.22 pre-warms the map at gateway startup, collapsing that overhead to ~5ms.

For [WhatsApp sales automation](/solutions/whatsapp-sales-automation) deployments where customers expect instant AI replies, this is transformative. A 20-second processing gap before the first response is enough to lose a lead. At 5ms, the agent feels live.

The release also lazy-loads startup-idle plugin work, reuses immutable plugin metadata snapshots, caches plugin SDK alias maps, and skips irrelevant macOS PATH probes — compounding the startup gains beyond just the auth map.

## Meeting Notes Plugin: AI That Knows What Was Discussed

The new Meeting Notes plugin ships as a source-only external plugin under the SDK source-provider contract. It supports:

- **Auto-start capture** — begins recording as soon as a meeting session opens
- **Manual transcript imports** — paste in transcripts from any external tool
- **Read-only CLI access** — query meeting history from the command line without modifying records
- **Discord voice as first live source** — real-time capture from Discord voice channels

For [B2B export AI SDR](/solutions/ai-sdr-for-b2b-export) teams, this opens a workflow that was previously impossible out of the box: the AI SDR can reference what was actually said on a discovery call when composing follow-up messages, proposals, or objection responses. No manual copy-paste of meeting notes into context.

Future releases are expected to add more live sources (Google Meet, Zoom, Teams).

## Claude 4.x Migration: Better AI Backbone at Scale

v2026.5.22 migrates 1M-context handling to GA-capable Claude 4.x models and automatically upgrades existing configurations. The retired model list includes old Groq, GitHub Copilot, OpenAI, and xAI catalog entries that accumulated over the past year.

For SDR deployments, the practical impact is threefold:

1. **Longer conversation memory** — 1M-context GA means the agent can hold a full prospect history without truncation
2. **Stability** — GA models have better uptime SLAs and fewer surprise deprecations
3. **Zero migration work** — existing configs are auto-upgraded on first boot

This pairs with the new `embeddingProviders` capability contract and registration API, which lets teams plug in custom vector-search backends for memory and RAG — critical for [multi-channel sales pipeline](/solutions/multi-channel-sales-pipeline) setups where prospect context spans WhatsApp, email, and Telegram threads.

## Sub-Agent Context Hardening

Default sub-agent bootstrap context is now scoped to `AGENTS.md` and `TOOLS.md` only. This reduces prompt bleed in multi-agent SDR pipelines where child agents were inadvertently inheriting full workspace context and generating off-topic responses.

For teams running the [B2B SDR Agent Template](https://github.com/iPythoning/b2b-sdr-agent-template), this is a net improvement — your sub-agents stay focused on their assigned task without needing manual context pruning.

## Windows & Docker: Enterprise Deployment Gets Easier

The Windows installer now fully bootstraps portable Git, Node.js, and pnpm when native tools are absent — eliminating the most common enterprise blocker where IT policies prevent pre-installing development toolchains. The Docker image pre-creates workspace and config mount points with correct `node` ownership, and (critically) no longer prints the Gateway bearer token in setup logs.

For [manufacturing AI sales agents](/solutions/ai-sales-agent-for-manufacturing) deployed on Windows-first enterprise infrastructure, this removes the last major friction point in on-premise deployments.

## Channel Reliability: 100+ Targeted Fixes

The fix list for this release is unusually dense. Highlights for sales channel operators:

| Channel | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| Telegram | Markdown tables preserved through chunking; local media sent instead of dropped; deduped replayed dispatches |
| Slack | Reasoning payloads suppressed before delivery; approval prompts stay in originating thread |
| WhatsApp | Shared-secret bearer callers can stream session history without scope header |
| All channels | `/verbose on` now correctly enables tool/progress summaries |

For [Telegram lead generation](/solutions/telegram-lead-generation) workflows, the markdown table fix alone is significant — structured pricing tables and comparison sheets were rendering as garbled text in previous versions.

## Before vs After: What Changes in Your SDR Deployment

| Area | Before v2026.5.22 | After v2026.5.22 |
|------|-------------------|------------------|
| First message latency | Up to 20s cold-start | ~5ms warm gateway |
| Meeting context | Manual copy-paste | Auto-captured via plugin |
| AI models | Mixed vintage catalog | Unified Claude 4.x GA |
| Windows deploy | Requires pre-installed toolchain | Self-bootstrapping installer |
| Telegram tables | Garbled on long messages | Preserved through chunking |
| Sub-agent bleed | Full workspace context inherited | Scoped to AGENTS.md + TOOLS.md |

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Does the 4,100× speedup require any configuration?**
No. It activates automatically on upgrade. The gateway pre-warms the auth-state map at startup with no additional config keys.

**Will my existing Claude model configs break after the 4.x migration?**
No. Existing configs are auto-upgraded on first boot. You will see a one-time migration log entry, but no manual action is required.

**Is the Meeting Notes plugin available on all deployment types?**
Yes — it ships as a source-only external plugin compatible with all OpenClaw deployment modes (local, Docker, managed). Discord voice is the first live source; additional sources will be added in future releases.

**What embedding providers can I plug in with the new API?**
The `embeddingProviders` API is provider-agnostic. Community integrations for OpenAI, Cohere, and local models (via Ollama) are already emerging in the ecosystem.

**Is the Gateway bearer token exposure in Docker logs a security issue I need to patch manually?**
Only if you captured Docker setup logs and stored them. The token in logs was introduced in an earlier version; v2026.5.22 suppresses it going forward. Rotate your token after upgrading if you have reason to believe logs were captured.

## Get Started Today

The fastest way to deploy a production-ready B2B SDR agent on OpenClaw v2026.5.22:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iPythoning/b2b-sdr-agent-template/main/install.sh | bash
```

This installs the full [B2B SDR Agent Template](https://github.com/iPythoning/b2b-sdr-agent-template) — 7-layer context system, 13 cron jobs, WhatsApp/Telegram/email channels, and the complete sales pipeline from lead capture to close.

Already running OpenClaw? Upgrade with:

```bash
clawhub update b2b-sdr-agent
```
